claims.claim: Policy MP-RAD-014 makes criteria 2.1-2.4 disjunctive; MRI is medically necessary if ANY ONE is met.
Member's low back pain persisted 13 weeks (onset 2026-04-06), exceeding the 6-week threshold in 2.1.
Member completed a full supervised PT course (16 visits over 8 weeks) with documented modalities.
PT was documented as failed: 15/16 visit adherence, minimal improvement, persistent right radicular symptoms.
NSAID pharmacotherapy (Naproxen 500mg BID) trialed for 9 weeks with adherence.
A muscle relaxant (Cyclobenzaprine) was also trialed and discontinued for sedation.
A supervised home exercise program was provided as part of conservative therapy.
Therefore the denial's sole factual premise (no documented 6-week conservative therapy trial) is contradicted by the chart, satisfying 2.1.
Section 2.3 provides an independent pathway: radicular pain ≥4 weeks with corresponding objective neurologic findings.
Radicular pain persisted 13 weeks with new lateral calf/foot numbness, exceeding the 4-week threshold.
Objective neurologic findings present: diminished right ankle DTR (1+ vs 2+, a change from prior symmetric exam).
Additional objective findings: right S1 dermatomal sensory loss and positive straight leg raise at 30 degrees on the right.
Treating physician's assessment: lumbar radiculopathy, right (M54.16), with new objective neurologic findings after failed conservative therapy.
The denial evaluated only 2.1 and failed to consider 2.3, a material omission.
The appeal is timely, filed within the 180-day written appeal window.
A peer-to-peer review with a plan medical director may be requested within 14 days.

missing_elements: Member date of birth is null/absent — payers routinely require DOB to validate the member; its omission could be used to reject or delay the appeal.
No physician wet/electronic signature or attestation — letter is signed by 'Patient Access Team' on behalf of Dr. Okafor rather than by the ordering physician.
No callback phone number, fax, or return address provided despite 'Please contact our office' request.
No provider NPI or facility identifiers (TIN/address) included.
Appeal level ('First-Level Appeal') is asserted although denial's appeal_level field was null — should be confirmed against payer rules.

needs_human.issue: Peer-to-peer 14-day window: the policy allows a P2P request within 14 days of the denial (denial 2026-07-06, so ~2026-07-20). Filing on 2026-07-18 leaves almost no margin — decide immediately whether to request P2P and confirm the offer is still open.
Member DOB is missing from the denial and letter — obtain and insert to avoid a procedural rejection for identity mismatch.
Confirm the letter should be signed/attested by Dr. Okafor rather than only the Patient Access Team; add signature block and date.
Add contact phone/fax and return address so the plan can schedule the P2P or respond.
Verify this is correctly the first-level appeal (denial appeal_level was null).

Cyclobenzaprine is characterized as a qualifying modality, but the policy names PT, NSAID/analgesic, or supervised HEP — muscle relaxant is not explicitly listed; keep as supporting color only, not as a standalone qualifier.

summary: This is a strong, well-cited appeal with two independent grounds: it directly rebuts the denial's factual premise under policy 2.1 (documented failed 6-week conservative therapy across all three named modalities) and raises an unevaluated independent pathway under 2.3 (radicular pain >4 weeks with objective neuro findings), both supported by verbatim chart and policy citations. It is timely (168 days to deadline). Before sending, a human should insert the member DOB, add the physician signature and a contact number, and urgently decide on the peer-to-peer request since the 14-day P2P window closes around 2026-07-20.
